CP00-613 is a sound descriptor-only RP20 Data Room/VDR pack. I independently recomputed the 150-unit / 8-section coverage and the full deliverable-type distribution (impl 77, ui 23, fixture 4, test 20, hermes 4, claude 7, contract 3, security 6, recovery 6) and confirmed they agree across the registry, service generator, validators, tests, validator script, contract JSON projections, manifest, and the live plan. All no-write / no-real-data / no-runtime boundaries hold — including the newly-introduced P02 service surface, which is properly attested as not opened — and the authority boundary keeps Claude read-only and non-approving with human approval gated. The pending adjudication/inspection/command-evidence files are the correct pre-review state.

Read-only inspection of registry.js/service.js/validators.js/index.js, the contract projections, model.test.js, the rp20 validator script, README, the live closeout-pack-plan, and the cp00-613 evidence artifacts confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is supported.

Verified: (1) Section rows produce exactly 150 units across 8 micro-sections (M06=16 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07=22 Test And Golden Case Set, M08=20 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09=20 Claude Review Packet, M10=10 Closeout And Next Handoff, P02.M00=20 Scope Inventory, P02.M01=20 Contract Draft, P02.M02=22 Type And Shape Definition); the manifest micro_phase_row_counts agree. (2) Per-row deliverable-type mapping was manually recomputed across all 8 sections and equals the plan's per-unit types, the validator's expected counts, and the manifest delivery_counts {implementation:77, ui:23, fixture:4, test:20, hermes_evidence:4, claude_review:7, contract:3, security_audit:6, failure_recovery:6} = 150. (3) The M06 boundary against CP612 is clean and non-overlapping: CP612 ends at RP20.P01.M06.S04 with next_subphase RP20.P01.M06.S05 = CP613 first unit; CP613 hands off to CP00-614 at RP20.P02.M03.S01, matching the plan. (4) contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json cp613_pack_binding, cp613_requirements, cp613_no_write_attestation, and the cp613 descriptor projection match the registry constants the tests deep-equal against. (5) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensive: freezeCp613Result and the CP613 no-write attestation set all data-room/vdr/rfi/cp/closing-binder/access-analytics dispatch, permission-decision, audit-trace, object-storage, persistence, lock, idempotency, failure-recovery, integration-smoke, and real-data flags to false; the CP613 validator additionally asserts executes_api_handler / executes_service_entrypoint_runtime / executes_request_normalization_runtime / executes_permission_precheck_runtime / executes_audit_hint_runtime / executes_failure_recovery_runtime / executes_integration_smoke_runtime are all false, properly attesting the new P02 service surface as not opened. (6) Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; validators only ever return production_ready_candidate, never a final ready claim, preserving the production_ready gate. (7) Hermes usage is deterministic descriptor packet/receipt shaping only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. (8) Review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1455-byte stdout, well-formed JSON, stderr 0), prompt is 2416 B (<25600) with no embedded diff/source, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools, no omitted active untracked implementation files; full source inspection was performed (summary-only correctly prohibited for this risk-C pack).

The pending evidence files (command-evidence.json commands:[], adjudication.md pending, construction-inspection.json PENDING_REVIEW, claude-review-result.json pending_review, manifest production_ready:false/runtime_ready:false/status:in_progress, all 150 units status in_progress) are the correct pre-adjudication state, identical to how the already-closed CP00-612 sibling looked before its review; they are populated downstream in the hardening sequence (normalize → receipt_validate → finding_adjudication → construction_inspection → final_closeout_validation → commit). Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room data/html, workbook/*, LDIP integration JSON, the v2.0 docx, new-session-handoff.md) are correctly out of pack scope per changed-file-scope.json and were not treated as part of CP00-613.

Limitation: as a read-only independent reviewer I cannot execute the closeout validation command matrix (npm test, npm run validate, npm run rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate, git diff --check, etc.). Source inspection indicates these would pass — the identically-shaped CP00-612 matrix passed at exit 0 across 2589 tests — but final pack closeout remains contingent on that matrix completing at exit 0 plus human final approval. Claude is an independent reviewer, not the final approver, and the enterprise trust claim is closed outside this receipt.
